invariants_checked: MEM-0050 typed data flow in impl-plan
MEM-0073 external sources are research inputs

summary: The plan keeps Codexter as normal Codex plus installed skills, scopes one coherent local invocation contract, names the concrete helper/skill/doc surfaces, and defers Symphony polling plus remote execution. The strongest tradeoff is accepting a small testable helper module while avoiding a public CLI or daemon.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The ticket solves one coherent problem: make Codexter invocable through normal Codex with stable request, routing, compute, and proof contracts.
The scope explicitly rejects the daemon, external board adapters, and public CLI shape that would blur the corrected architecture.
rubric_sections.next_action: Approve or refine the contract boundary, then move the ticket to building.

rubric_sections.name: implementation-plan
findings: The file map and signature deltas are concrete enough for a builder to start without inventing the module boundaries.
The typed flow example makes the request-to-proof path inspectable and keeps normal Codex, existing skills, and the helper module in their own lanes.
rubric_sections.next_action: During build, keep helper subcommands diagnostic-only and make the skill package the public invocation surface.

next_action: Await plan approval, then implement TASK-0107 as one local Codex invocation contract ticket.
